stories: Good evening Sean, it's Tuesday evening, the twenty-sixth of May
And here's a fun one to start вЂ” today marks the global release of Grand Theft Auto Six
Rockstar has kept us waiting since 2013, and it finally landed today
Your calendar for tomorrow, Wednesday the twenty-seventh, looks clear вЂ” so you have a wide open day ahead
On Singapore, some solid economic news today
The government revised first-quarter GDP growth sharply higher вЂ” up to six percent, from the four-point-six in the advance estimate
Inflation also came in below expectations: headline at one-point-eight percent for April, versus the two percent forecast, and core at one-point-four
The Ministry of Trade and Industry projects full-year growth between two and four percent, though they flagged energy disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz as a continuing risk
Good morning Sean, it's Tuesday, the twenty-sixth of May
Fun fact: on this date in 1896 - a hundred and thirty years ago today - Charles Dow published the Dow Jones Industrial Average for the very first time
Twelve stocks, opening value of forty point nine four
Nine o'clock is the Weekly Integrations PwC meeting on Teams, then bi-monthly catch-up with Rahul at nine-thirty
Ten to eleven is the GBS T&AS Monthly MOP and MIP
Deb joins you for thirty minutes at eleven, Tax Diagnostic at eleven-thirty, then lunch with Wilfred in the lobby of Tower Two at midday
On Singapore: construction at Chong Pang City resumes today after three weeks paused - a metal bar had fallen onto a nearby HDB roof
Singapore is also quietly rezoning Lim Chu Kang agricultural land for defence while shifting adjacent military land toward farming

Fun fact to start the evening: today is Geek Pride Day ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” chosen because May twenty-fifth marks the anniversary of the original Star Wars release in 1977

On the Singapore front today, some encouraging economic data
April inflation came in lower than expected at one point eight percent, and first-quarter GDP was revised sharply upward to six percent, from an earlier estimate of four point six
The Monetary Authority expects full-year inflation to stay between one point five and two point five percent
